original_snippet_context: The author discusses the benefits of exchange rate stabilization as observed in Argentina and contrasts it with Brazil's situation. He criticizes those who attributed the fall of Brazilian bonds to the proposed Caixa de Conversão while highlighting the general decline in bond values due to increased discount rates by the Bank of England.
relevant_quote: «Olhe meu amigo,» disse-me elle, «é a lei mais sabia que se tem feito em meu paiz...Hoje tudo desapareceu. Os descontos cahiram a 4 %, os juros são baixos e o capital abundante, o que nos permitte competir vantajosamente com os nossos concurrentes nos mercados extrangeiros, levando para alli os cereaes, a carne, o assucar, etc., desenvolvendo nossa viação e instrucção, e consolidando nosso credito.
justification: The author presents Argentina's fixed exchange rate as a model, emphasizing its positive impact on agriculture, industry, trade, capital inflow, and reduced interest rates—all arguments aligned with the expansionist view.
